<div class="quiz-shell edu-math-trigonometry-checkpoint" data-default-total="4"></div>

<script>
  (() => {
    const DEFAULT_TOPIC = "unit-circle";
    const BANKS = {
      "radians-and-degrees": [degreesRadiansQuestion, coterminalQuestion, referenceAngleQuestion, quadrantQuestion],
      "unit-circle": [coordinateQuestion, signsQuestion, specialAngleQuestion, tangentQuestion],
      "sine-cosine": [sineCoordinateQuestion, cosineCoordinateQuestion, rightTriangleQuestion, signQuestion],
      "trig-functions-and-graphs": [amplitudeQuestion, periodQuestion, midlineQuestion, phaseQuestion],
      identities: [pythagoreanIdentityQuestion, tangentIdentityQuestion, reciprocalQuestion, verifyIdentityQuestion],
      "trig-equations": [basicSineEquationQuestion, basicCosineEquationQuestion, periodicQuestion, intervalQuestion],
      applications: [rightTriangleQuestion, elevationQuestion, sinusoidQuestion, bearingQuestion],
      "trigonometry-cumulative-review": [degreesRadiansQuestion, coordinateQuestion, specialAngleQuestion, amplitudeQuestion, pythagoreanIdentityQuestion, basicSineEquationQuestion],
    };

    document.querySelectorAll(".edu-math-trigonometry-checkpoint:not([data-ready])").forEach(init);

    function init(root) {
      const config = readConfig(root);
      const state = { config, total: config.defaultTotal, index: 0, score: 0 };
      root.dataset.ready = "true";
      renderStart(root, state);
    }

    function readConfig(root) {
      const node = root.previousElementSibling;
      let parsed = {};
      if (node?.classList?.contains("edu-math-trigonometry-checkpoint-config")) {
        try {
          parsed = JSON.parse(node.textContent || "{}");
        } catch {
          parsed = {};
        }
      }
      return {
        topic: parsed.topic || root.dataset.topic || DEFAULT_TOPIC,
        title: parsed.title || "Trigonometry checkpoint",
        defaultTotal: Math.max(1, Math.min(20, Number(parsed.defaultTotal || 4))),
      };
    }

    function renderStart(root, state) {
      root.replaceChildren();
      const input = element("input", { class: "quiz-count", type: "number", min: "1", max: "20", value: String(state.total) });
      const button = element("button", { type: "button" }, "Start checkpoint");
      button.addEventListener("click", () => {
        state.total = Math.max(1, Math.min(20, Number(input.value) || 4));
        state.index = 0;
        state.score = 0;
        renderQuestion(root, state, makeQuestion(state.config.topic));
      });
      root.append(
        element("p", { class: "quiz-topic" }, state.config.title),
        element("div", { class: "quiz-start" }, element("label", {}, element("span", {}, "Number of questions"), input), button),
      );
    }

    function renderQuestion(root, state, question) {
      root.replaceChildren();
      let answered = false;
      const progress = element(
        "div",
        { class: "quiz-progress" },
        element("span", {}, `Question ${state.index + 1} of ${state.total}`),
        element("span", {}, `Score: ${state.score}/${state.total}`),
      );
      const feedback = element("p", { class: "quiz-feedback", "aria-live": "polite" });
      const next = element("button", { type: "button", disabled: "" }, "Next question");
      const options = radioList(question.options, (selected) => {
        if (answered) return;
        answered = true;
        const correct = selected === question.answer;
        if (correct) state.score += 1;
        feedback.className = correct ? "quiz-feedback correct" : "quiz-feedback wrong";
        feedback.replaceChildren(correct ? "Correct." : `Not quite. The answer is ${question.answer}.`, " ", question.explanation);
        options.querySelectorAll("input").forEach((input) => {
          input.disabled = true;
        });
        progress.lastElementChild.textContent = `Score: ${state.score}/${state.total}`;
        next.disabled = false;
      });
      next.addEventListener("click", () => {
        state.index += 1;
        if (state.index >= state.total) renderDone(root, state);
        else renderQuestion(root, state, makeQuestion(state.config.topic));
      });
      root.append(progress, stimulus(question.stimulus), element("p", { class: "quiz-prompt" }, question.prompt), feedback, options, next);
    }

    function renderDone(root, state) {
      root.replaceChildren();
      const restart = element("button", { type: "button" }, "Try again");
      restart.addEventListener("click", () => renderStart(root, state));
      root.append(element("h3", {}, "Checkpoint complete"), element("p", {}, `Final score: ${state.score}/${state.total}`), restart);
    }

    function makeQuestion(topic) {
      const bank = BANKS[topic] || BANKS[DEFAULT_TOPIC];
      return choice(bank)();
    }

    function degreesRadiansQuestion() {
      return question({ prompt: "Convert 180 degrees to radians.", stimulus: "half turn", answer: "pi", distractors: ["2pi", "pi/2", "180pi"], explanation: "A full turn is 2pi radians, so a half turn is pi radians." });
    }
    function coterminalQuestion() {
      return question({ prompt: "Which angle is coterminal with -30 degrees in [0,360)?", stimulus: "add 360 degrees", answer: "330 degrees", distractors: ["30 degrees", "300 degrees", "-390 degrees"], explanation: "-30 + 360 = 330." });
    }
    function referenceAngleQuestion() {
      return question({ prompt: "What is the reference angle for 150 degrees?", stimulus: "Quadrant II", answer: "30 degrees", distractors: ["60 degrees", "150 degrees", "210 degrees"], explanation: "In Quadrant II, reference angle is 180 - theta." });
    }
    function quadrantQuestion() {
      return question({ prompt: "Which quadrant contains 240 degrees?", stimulus: "between 180 and 270", answer: "III", distractors: ["I", "II", "IV"], explanation: "Angles between 180 and 270 degrees are in Quadrant III." });
    }
    function coordinateQuestion() {
      return question({ prompt: "On the unit circle, which coordinate is sine?", stimulus: "point = (cos theta, sin theta)", answer: "vertical coordinate", distractors: ["horizontal coordinate", "radius length", "angle measure"], explanation: "Sine is the y-coordinate." });
    }
    function signsQuestion() {
      return question({ prompt: "In Quadrant II, what are the signs of sine and cosine?", stimulus: "x negative, y positive", answer: "sine positive, cosine negative", distractors: ["both positive", "both negative", "sine negative, cosine positive"], explanation: "Sine is y, cosine is x." });
    }
    function specialAngleQuestion() {
      return question({ prompt: "What is sin(30 degrees)?", stimulus: "special angle", answer: "1/2", distractors: ["sqrt(3)/2", "sqrt(2)/2", "1"], explanation: "The 30-60-90 triangle gives sin(30)=1/2." });
    }
    function tangentQuestion() {
      return question({ prompt: "What is tan(theta) in terms of sine and cosine?", stimulus: "tangent ratio", answer: "sin(theta)/cos(theta)", distractors: ["cos(theta)/sin(theta)", "1/sin(theta)", "sin(theta)+cos(theta)"], explanation: "Tangent is sine divided by cosine when cosine is not zero." });
    }
    function sineCoordinateQuestion() {
      return question({ prompt: "At 90 degrees, what is sine?", stimulus: "unit-circle point (0,1)", answer: "1", distractors: ["0", "-1", "undefined"], explanation: "The y-coordinate at 90 degrees is 1." });
    }
    function cosineCoordinateQuestion() {
      return question({ prompt: "At 180 degrees, what is cosine?", stimulus: "unit-circle point (-1,0)", answer: "-1", distractors: ["1", "0", "undefined"], explanation: "The x-coordinate at 180 degrees is -1." });
    }
    function rightTriangleQuestion() {
      return question({ prompt: "In a right triangle, sine equals which ratio?", stimulus: "SOH-CAH-TOA", answer: "opposite / hypotenuse", distractors: ["adjacent / hypotenuse", "opposite / adjacent", "hypotenuse / opposite"], explanation: "SOH means sine = opposite over hypotenuse." });
    }
    function signQuestion() {
      return question({ prompt: "At 300 degrees, is sine positive or negative?", stimulus: "Quadrant IV", answer: "negative", distractors: ["positive", "zero", "undefined"], explanation: "Quadrant IV has negative y-values, so sine is negative." });
    }
    function amplitudeQuestion() {
      return question({ prompt: "What is the amplitude of y = 3 sin(x)?", stimulus: "vertical stretch", answer: "3", distractors: ["1", "2pi", "pi"], explanation: "Amplitude is the absolute value of the coefficient in front of sine or cosine." });
    }
    function periodQuestion() {
      return question({ prompt: "What is the period of sin(x)?", stimulus: "basic sine wave", answer: "2pi", distractors: ["pi", "1", "4pi"], explanation: "Basic sine repeats every 2pi radians." });
    }
    function midlineQuestion() {
      return question({ prompt: "What is the midline of y = sin(x) + 4?", stimulus: "vertical shift", answer: "y = 4", distractors: ["y = 1", "y = -4", "x = 4"], explanation: "The +4 shifts the center line to y=4." });
    }
    function phaseQuestion() {
      return question({ prompt: "In y = sin(x - pi/2), which way is the graph shifted?", stimulus: "inside subtraction", answer: "right", distractors: ["left", "up", "down"], explanation: "Subtracting inside shifts the graph right." });
    }
    function pythagoreanIdentityQuestion() {
      return question({ prompt: "Complete the identity: sin^2(x) + cos^2(x) = ?", stimulus: "Pythagorean identity", answer: "1", distractors: ["0", "tan(x)", "2"], explanation: "The unit-circle point has distance 1 from the origin." });
    }
    function tangentIdentityQuestion() {
      return question({ prompt: "Which identity rewrites tan(x)?", stimulus: "quotient identity", answer: "sin(x)/cos(x)", distractors: ["cos(x)/sin(x)", "1/cos(x)", "1/sin(x)"], explanation: "tan(x)=sin(x)/cos(x)." });
    }
    function reciprocalQuestion() {
      return question({ prompt: "Which reciprocal identity is correct?", stimulus: "secant", answer: "sec(x)=1/cos(x)", distractors: ["sec(x)=1/sin(x)", "sec(x)=sin(x)/cos(x)", "sec(x)=cos(x)"], explanation: "Secant is reciprocal cosine." });
    }
    function verifyIdentityQuestion() {
      return question({ prompt: "What is the safest first move when proving a trig identity?", stimulus: "identity strategy", answer: "rewrite one side using known identities", distractors: ["divide by zero", "change both sides randomly", "plug in one angle only"], explanation: "Use known identities and algebra to transform one side into the other." });
    }
    function basicSineEquationQuestion() {
      return question({ prompt: "In [0,360), solve sin(theta)=0.", stimulus: "x-axis intersections", answer: "0 and 180 degrees", distractors: ["90 and 270 degrees", "30 and 150 degrees", "45 and 225 degrees"], explanation: "Sine is y-coordinate, which is 0 on the x-axis." });
    }
    function basicCosineEquationQuestion() {
      return question({ prompt: "In [0,360), solve cos(theta)=0.", stimulus: "y-axis intersections", answer: "90 and 270 degrees", distractors: ["0 and 180 degrees", "30 and 330 degrees", "45 and 315 degrees"], explanation: "Cosine is x-coordinate, which is 0 on the y-axis." });
    }
    function periodicQuestion() {
      return question({ prompt: "Why can sin(theta)=1/2 have infinitely many solutions?", stimulus: "periodicity", answer: "sine repeats every full turn", distractors: ["sine is linear", "angles stop at 360", "cosine is undefined"], explanation: "Adding 360 degrees or 2pi radians gives coterminal solutions." });
    }
    function intervalQuestion() {
      return question({ prompt: "In [0,360), solve sin(theta)=1.", stimulus: "top of unit circle", answer: "90 degrees", distractors: ["0 degrees", "180 degrees", "270 degrees"], explanation: "The top of the unit circle is (0,1)." });
    }
    function elevationQuestion() {
      return question({ prompt: "For angle of elevation, opposite side usually represents what?", stimulus: "right-triangle model", answer: "height", distractors: ["horizontal distance", "hypotenuse always", "angle measure"], explanation: "Height is vertical, opposite the ground angle." });
    }
    function sinusoidQuestion() {
      return question({ prompt: "Which trig function type models repeating seasonal behavior?", stimulus: "periodic data", answer: "sinusoidal", distractors: ["linear", "quadratic", "constant only"], explanation: "Sine and cosine model smooth repeating cycles." });
    }
    function bearingQuestion() {
      return question({ prompt: "A vector with components (3,4) has length?", stimulus: "right triangle", answer: "5", distractors: ["7", "1", "12"], explanation: "Use the Pythagorean theorem: sqrt(3^2+4^2)=5." });
    }

    function question({ prompt, stimulus, answer, distractors, explanation }) {
      return { prompt, stimulus, answer, options: shuffle([answer, ...distractors]), explanation };
    }
    function radioList(options, onSelect) {
      const name = `trigonometry-checkpoint-${Math.random().toString(36).slice(2)}`;
      const container = element("div", { class: "quiz-options" });
      options.forEach((option) => {
        const input = element("input", { type: "radio", name, value: option });
        input.addEventListener("change", () => onSelect(option));
        container.append(element("label", {}, input, element("span", {}, option)));
      });
      return container;
    }
    function stimulus(text) {
      return element("pre", { class: "quiz-stimulus" }, text);
    }
    function element(tag, attrs = {}, ...children) {
      const node = document.createElement(tag);
      Object.entries(attrs).forEach(([key, value]) => node.setAttribute(key, value));
      node.append(...children);
      return node;
    }
    function choice(values) {
      return values[Math.floor(Math.random() * values.length)];
    }
    function shuffle(values) {
      const copy = values.slice();
      for (let index = copy.length - 1; index > 0; index -= 1) {
        const other = Math.floor(Math.random() * (index + 1));
        [copy[index], copy[other]] = [copy[other], copy[index]];
      }
      return copy;
    }
  })();
</script>
